source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We are looking for an Account Executive to join our Mid-Market sales team in the United States. You will be part of a growing sales team on the ground, working with an award-winning product in an industry that is more crucial than ever before. You'll get to work with large global organizations and help them to build and foster a more cyber-secure company culture.

As an Account Executive, your responsibilities are to build and manage a strategic sales pipeline through cold-prospecting into target companies, close new business, and hit your sales quota. We'll support and supply you with the tools and frameworks for you to successfully grow our presence in the area.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and manage a strategic pipeline of commercial opportunities through proactive outbound prospecting.

 - Own the full sales cycle from discovery to close, navigating complex buying processes.

 - Partner closely with SDR and Marketing teams to drive pipeline generation and account strategy.

 - Develop a deep understanding of prospect business challenges and position Hoxhunt’s solutions effectively.

 - Consistently meet and exceed sales targets, driving measurable growth in the region.

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In your first 30 days, you will dive deep into Hoxhunt's product, value proposition, and sales motion, while building strong collaborative relationships with our SDR and Marketing teams.

 - By day 60, you will be actively driving your own pipeline through proactive outbound prospecting, running discovery calls, and managing early-stage mid-market opportunities.

 - By day 90 and beyond, you will be independently navigating buying processes, consistently closing deals, and contributing to the overall growth of our North American presence.

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven SaaS Sales Expertise: 3+ years experience in B2B SaaS sales or business development, with a track record of exceeding targets.

 - Customer-Centric Mindset: Genuine interest in understanding prospect challenges and connecting solutions to real business needs.

 - Hunter Mentality & Drive: Goal-oriented, energized by building pipeline and closing new business.

 - Quick Learner: Ability to grasp new concepts and articulate value clearly and effectively.

 - Strong Communicator: Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust quickly.

 


BONUS POINTS

 - Highly autonomous and self-motivated, thriving in a scale-up environment where you take full ownership of your success.

 - Ambitious and results-driven, with a passion for growing a market presence.

 - Skilled at understanding customer needs quickly and translating them into impactful solutions.

 - Resilient and resourceful, able to adapt strategies when facing challenges.

 - A true team player who embodies a “succeed together” mindset.

 


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

At Hoxhunt, we don't just build software. We build behavior change on a global scale. With a world-class product and over 50% year-on-year growth, we're helping companies around the world create a culture of cybersecurity, one employee at a time.

You’ll join a high-performing team focused on growing our presence in North America. The Minneapolis office is a hub for collaboration, where people work closely together, share ideas openly, and support each other in getting results. It’s a hands-on environment where you’ll have visibility into the business and the opportunity to make an impact early.

 
 


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: Annual

source_jobs.title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

At Hoxhunt, reliability is not a support function, it is a product enabler. As we scale globally, every release, every system interaction, and every user experience depends on infrastructure that is secure, resilient, and always available. This role is critical in making that possible. You will ensure our platform can grow without friction, enabling teams to move fast while safeguarding trust, compliance, and performance at every step.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the reliability and scalability of our cloud infrastructure on Google Cloud Platform

 - Lead the evolution of Kubernetes-based environments across development and production

 - Partner with product, platform, and security teams to enable fast, safe, and repeatable deployments

 - Build and operate observability systems that provide real-time insight and actionable signals

 - Drive proactive reliability improvements by identifying and eliminating risks early

 - Deliver automation that reduces manual work and increases engineering velocity

 - Improve and scale global production environments in a compliance-heavy landscape

 - Support and mentor teams in adopting SRE and DevOps best practices

 - Collaborate across engineering to embed security, resilience, and reliability by default


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

During your first 3 months, you’ll get into our systems and architecture, contribute to infrastructure improvements, and actively support reliability and incident response efforts.

By month 6, you’ll own key areas of our infrastructure, deliver measurable improvements in observability and automation, and enable teams to ship with greater confidence.



WHAT HELPS YOU THRIVE AT HOXHUNT

You have:

 - 5+ years of experience in SRE, DevOps, or similar infrastructure-focused roles

 - Strong experience with cloud platforms (GCP preferred, AWS or Azure also relevant)

 - Hands-on experience running Kubernetes in production

 - Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Helm, Kustomize)

 - Strong knowledge of modern CI/CD and cloud-native ecosystems

 - Proficiency in at least one programming language (JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, or Python)

 - Deep understanding of distributed systems and systems thinking

 - Proven ability to troubleshoot complex production incidents and drive resolution

 - Comfort working cross-functionally and guiding teams toward reliable engineering practices

 - AI-native mindset: you actively use AI tools (LLMs, agents) in your daily workflows to accelerate problem-solving, automation, and delivery — while applying strong judgment, validation, and security best practices

Bonus points if you also:

 - Have built or owned observability platforms at scale

 - Have experience in regulated or compliance-heavy environments

 - Have worked in fast-growing SaaS or scale-up environments

 - Enjoy simplifying complex systems and improving developer experience



WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

You’ll join a high-ownership SRE team operating at the intersection of platform, product, and security. The team combines strong technical depth with a collaborative, low-ego mindset. Feedback is direct, support is real, and success is shared. You’ll work closely with engineers across Hoxhunt, helping them build, ship, and scale reliably while continuously learning from one another.



WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US?

Compensation: Monthly salary of €6500–€7500 depending on your experience.

Working ways: We work in a flexible, but hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office weekly.

Hig

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, New product, Threat Detection
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.

Why this role matters

We’re growing fast, over 50% year-over-year, and expanding our product offerings.

Join us in building a new product that protects enterprise users from cyber attacks through real-time threat detection, while giving organizations continuous visibility into their risk surface and attack vectors.

The product is already seeing strong market demand, and we're looking for you to help shape it and define how it scales as we grow.

What you’ll own and drive

 - Own delivery of key features end-to-end across the technology stack from building detection for emerging cyber attack patterns and large scale data processing to building intuitive, high quality user experiences.

 - Contributing to team processes and practices to help us scale the team and product

 - Improve our system reliability and overall architecture by coordinating execution plans and making key technical decisions.

 - Drive and mentor the team through technical leadership, acting as a multiplier who unblocks peers and leads insightful code reviews to solve complex challenges.

What makes you thrive here

You’ll probably have at least five years’ experience working in the industry, but we care less about how long you’ve been active and more about what you’ve been doing. We’d love to hear about what you’ve worked on, how it was built, and why it was a success.

You have

 - a proven track record of leading complex technical projects from end to finish

 - a strong product mindset, with the ability to turn ambiguous problems into user-focused requirements and value generation

 - deep, hands-on experience with TypeScript and React, along with experience building backend services and APIs. We use Node.js and MongoDB.

 - a solid understanding of the web platform

Who you’ll work with

You’ll be joining a team of ambitious product engineers who value high autonomy, low hierarchy and user-centered solutions. We are product-minded and work in agile, AI-native ways without compromising quality. Our goal is to build the best product in the market and win while enjoying the journey and creating the best working environment for each other.

Join us in building a great product and having fun along the way!

What you can expect from us

 - Compensation: Monthly salary of €6000-€7500 depending on your experience. You may notice varying salary ranges for roles with similar titles across Hoxhunt; this is because each range is grounded in our role leveling and reflects the seniority, scope, and impact expectations required for that specific role and team, and we operate with a low hierarchy.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, but hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office weekly.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We bring an incredibly driven, high-impact energy to our work, but we leave our egos at the door. You will be surrounded by wildly talented, dedicated colleagues in an environment built on extreme kindness, support, and psychological safety.

 - Authentic trust & autonomy: We hire great people and trust them to do great work. You will find a culture free of micromanagement, giving you the autonomy to take real ownership, drive impact, and shape things early on.

 - A product you can be proud of: It is incredibly rare in cybersecurity to build a multi-award-winning product that end-users genuinely love. You will join a fast-paced, technically sophisticated team making a real, measurable impact against cybercrime, backed by strong financial stability and growth.

 - The perks that ma

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Financial Controller
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

We are scaling our finance function to support our next stage of growth. As Financial Controller, you will play a key role in external reporting and contribute to the development of our financial systems and tools as we expand globally across the US, EMEA, and APAC. You'll work closely with function leads, the management team, our external accountant, and our global reporting team, helping strengthen the financial foundation that supports our mission of protecting organizations from cyber threats.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN AND DRIVE

 - Manage external reporting and help ensure compliance with regulatory requirements

 - Build and enhance financial systems and tools to scale with business growth, including leading implementation projects (NetSuite, Power BI)

 - Drive improvements in controlling and reporting processes, both internal and external

 - Partner with our external accountant and global reporting team to align processes and reporting standards

 - Work with Sales and stakeholders globally to ensure accurate vendor registration, invoicing, and accounting records

 - Provide guidance on finance-related inquiries across the company

 - Support accounts receivable and collection processes, monitoring aging balances

 - Contribute to ARR reporting, maintaining and reconciling Salesforce and NetSuite data in collaboration with RevOps

 - Proactively introduce AI-native ways of working, identifying and adopting AI tools to improve financial processes and efficiency

 - Coach colleagues in financial processes and tools, fostering a culture of financial awareness across the company




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In your first 3 months you'll: Get up to speed on our external reporting processes, build strong partnerships with stakeholders and external accountants, and begin identifying opportunities to improve our controlling processes.

 - By month 6 you'll: Be contributing to improvements in our financial systems like NetSuite and Power BI, delivering accurate and compliant reporting, and helping strengthen company-wide financial literacy and operational efficiency.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

 - You have 2 to 4 years of relevant working experience in controlling and financial reporting

 - Excellent knowledge of financial processes, controlling, and reporting

 - Strong interest in developing controlling and reporting processes in a global setting

 - Hands-on experience with Netsuite and business intelligence tools (preferably Power BI, including implementation projects)

 - AI-native mindset, with a genuine interest in adopting AI tools to improve financial processes and efficiency

 - Top-tier Excel skills

 - Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to coach and collaborate across functions and cultures

Bonus points if you also:

 - Have experience working in a fast-growing SaaS or technology scale-up environment




WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH

We are proactive, independent, and solution-oriented doers who spot opportunities for improvement and take initiative. We are humble, unafraid to ask for help, and ready to tackle both routine tasks and major projects with equal enthusiasm. You will be expected to work confidently with C-level stakeholders, raising critical issues when needed while maintaining a strong grasp of the bigger business picture.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: Monthly salary of €4000 - €5500 depending on your experience.
   Working ways: We work in a flexible, hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office w

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative, APAC
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.






ABOUT THIS ROLE

We are looking for a Sales Development Representative to join our team in Singapore. This is a critical role in driving our APAC growth, helping us connect with new customers and expand our presence in a rapidly growing cybersecurity market.

As a Sales Development Representative at Hoxhunt, you’ll focus on generating new business opportunities through outbound prospecting and booking meetings for our Account Executives. You’ll work closely with both Sales and Marketing, building the foundation for pipeline growth while developing the skills needed to grow into a high-performing sales professional.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Generate pipeline through proactive outbound prospecting across phone, email, LinkedIn, and events.

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads, identifying strong opportunities for the sales team.

 - Book high-quality meetings for Account Executives to support revenue growth.

 - Partner closely with Marketing and Sales to align on messaging, campaigns, and target accounts.

 - Continuously refine your outreach approach based on feedback, data, and results.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your first 3 months you'll: move through structured onboarding, get fluent in the Hoxhunt value proposition, and start holding your own in conversations with senior security stakeholders while building a steady stream of qualified meetings.

By month 6 you'll: be consistently hitting your pipeline targets, navigating complex enterprise accounts with confidence, and working in true lockstep with your Account Executives across the region.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 1 to 3 years of outbound B2B sales or SDR experience, ideally in SaaS, cybersecurity, or technology, with a track record of consistently meeting or exceeding your targets

 - You welcome feedback and use it to continuously improve your approach

 - You prepare thoroughly for every conversation because the details are what make a pitch land

 - You understand the APAC market at a cultural level, not just a sales-process level, and relationship-building comes naturally to you

 - You communicate clearly and concisely in English, and can hold your own in a peer-level conversation with a technical decision-maker

 - You bring resilience and steady self-motivation to your work, staying consistent whether the day is easy or challenging

 - You genuinely care about the people you engage with, not just the outcome, and it shows in how you show up




BONUS POINTS

 - Have proficiency with modern sales tech stacks like ZoomInfo, Sales Navigator, Lusha and advanced CRM workflows

 - Have proficiency in an additional APAC language




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: Competitive base salary depending on your experience, plus a highly competitive performance-based commission model that rewards the impact you drive, uncapped.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, hybrid work setting. You are expected to collaborate from our Singapore office.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We bring an incredibly driven, high-impact energy to our work, but we leave our egos at the door. You will be surrounded by wildly talented, dedicated colleagues in an environment built on extreme kindness, support, and psychological safety.

 - Authentic trust & autonomy: We hire great people and trust them to do great work. You will find a culture free of micromanagement, giving you the autonomy to take real ownership, drive impact, and shape things early on.

 - A product you can be proud of: It is incredibly rare in c

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer, Support
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.



WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Support is changing fundamentally.

At Hoxhunt, we are transforming our Support organization from a reactive support function into a technology-led, AI-powered operation where automation and engineering are embedded directly into how support is delivered and scaled.

As an AI Engineer, you will help build that future from scratch. You will design and develop intelligent support systems that improve automated resolution rates, reduce repetitive manual work, and proactively identify customer friction before it becomes a larger problem.

Unlike many AI engineering roles focused primarily on internal tooling, this role sits much closer to the customer experience itself. The systems you build will directly shape how customers experience Hoxhunt Support at scale.

You’ll join the Support organization while also collaborating closely with our central AI automation team, helping shape broader AI-native ways of working across the company.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN AND DRIVE

 - Build intelligent AI agents and automation systems that transform how Support operates at scale.

 - Design AI-driven workflows for ticket triage, routing, escalations, automated resolution, and proactive issue detection.

 - Develop customer-facing support tooling and workflows that improve support quality, speed, and consistency.

 - Partner closely with Support, Product, and Engineering teams to solve operational and customer pain points through scalable technical solutions.

Continuously explore and adopt new AI tooling, agentic development workflows, and engineering approaches that improve operational scalability and customer experience.



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your first three months, you will build a deep understanding of Hoxhunt’s support operations, tooling, workflows, and customer pain points. You’ll begin shipping initial automations, support tooling, and AI workflows that create visible operational improvements for both customers and the Support team.

By month six, you will independently own larger support automation initiatives end-to-end, improving automated resolution rates, reducing repetitive support work, and helping scale the Support organization more efficiently.



WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You probably already have a strong software engineering foundation, but more importantly, you are excited about using AI to solve real customer and operational problems.

You have:

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals and experience building production-grade systems.

 - Hands-on experience with agentic coding tools such as Claude Code or similar AI-native development environments.

 - Experience building applications, integrations, automations, or internal tooling using technologies such as TypeScript/Node.js/React or Python.

 - A customer-centric mindset and genuine interest in improving customer experiences through technology.

 - The ability to thrive in fast-moving and ambiguous environments while maintaining structured, high-quality execution.

 - Curiosity about AI-native software development combined with strong collaboration skills and a passion for solving real operational problems through technology.

 - Experience with support platforms such as Zendesk, Pylon, or similar customer support tooling is a plus.



WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

You will officially join Hoxhunt’s Support organization, a team of 13 people split across Finland and the United States focused on delivering exceptional customer experiences at scale.

You’ll work especially closely with the Support team while col

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, New product, Full-stack
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This is a key role in building Hoxhunt's new product, helping organizations to see, understand, and act on human risks.

Your mission: make risk insights clear and actionable. You'll ship new features and improve parts that matter the most - APIs, high-throughput event processing pipeline, a flexible data visualization system and a powerful automation builder.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN AND DRIVE

Features are shipped end-to-end, together. Team own defining scope and architecture, building it and ensuring code runs smooth in production.

Lead the architecture and development of our systems. Raise the bar around you through code reviews, pair programming, and knowledge sharing. Tech stack: TypeScript/Node.js, AlloyDB/PostgreSQL, GraphQL, React, GCP.

Decide what to build by understanding the user problem and the business impact. Evaluate tradeoffs and use real data to iterate.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In the first 90 days, you have shipped important features end-to-end and you're influencing product and technical decisions.

By month six, you own one or more subsystems, defining the technical direction, what we build next, what we stop doing, what we invest in.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

Backend focused full-stack engineer who has worked with multiple products, shipping scalable cloud products end-to-end. Likely 5+ years of full-stack experience, but we care more about what you've built than the number.

Fluent in TypeScript, Node.js, and SQL databases or other similar technologies.

Genuinely care about the product and its users. Under ambiguity, you’ll collaborate with the whole team to figure out the right things rather than wait for a spec.

Experience with AI coding tools, or you're interested in building the setup that makes them effective.

Bonus points if you also:

 - Experience with GraphQL, React architecture or event-driven architecture.

 - Hands-on experience GCP, e.g. Pub/Sub or BigQuery




WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

You will be joining a small, mission-driven, and highly autonomous product team: six engineers, one designer, one PM.

We own the new product end-to-end. We want ship fast in small increments: something a customer can use now, then iterate.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: The monthly compensation range for this position is €6,000-7,500 / month depending on your experience. You may notice varying salary ranges for roles with similar titles across Hoxhunt; this is because each range is grounded in our role leveling and reflects the seniority, scope, and impact expectations required for that specific role and team, and we operate with a low hierarchy.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, but hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office weekly.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We bring an incredibly driven, high-impact energy to our work, but we leave our egos at the door. You will be surrounded by wildly talented, dedicated colleagues in an environment built on extreme kindness, support, and psychological safety.

 - Authentic trust & autonomy: We hire great people and trust them to do great work. You will find a culture free of micromanagement, giving you the autonomy to take real ownership, drive impact, and shape things early on.

 - A product you can be proud of: It is incredibly rare in cybersecurity to build a product that end-users genuinely love. You will join a fast-paced, technically sophisticated team making a real, measurable impact against cybercrime, backed by strong financial stab

source_jobs.title: Technical Support Engineer (French-speaking)
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

As a Technical Support Engineer, you will play a critical role in safeguarding our customers' digital assets and ensuring they get the most out of the Hoxhunt platform. We prioritize quality and unparalleled support, and you will be the dedicated go-to expert for our Francophone market. By resolving complex integration challenges and identity workflows, you directly empower our customers to maintain a strong security posture in a challenging cyber environment.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN AND DRIVE

 - Provide high-quality technical support to customers through email and our support platform, investigating and resolving issues across the full Hoxhunt product surface — from platform configuration and user management to integrations, email infrastructure, and security tooling.

 - Troubleshoot integration challenges between Hoxhunt and customers' identity providers, email environments, and security stack, including Active Directory, Okta, Microsoft 365, and Google Workspace.

 - Diagnose and resolve email deliverability and security configuration issues, ensuring Hoxhunt works reliably within customers' environments regardless of how their infrastructure is set up.

 - Support customers with identity and access management workflows, including SSO, SCIM provisioning, and authentication troubleshooting.

 - Act as the primary technical support contact for the Francophone market, combining deep product knowledge with clear, confident communication in French.

 - Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, and Customer Success to escalate and resolve complex issues, and to feed customer insight back into the product.

 - Contribute to self-service tooling, knowledge base quality, and initiatives that improve how the team scales.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your first 3 months you'll gain a deep understanding of our product, integrations, and key use cases. You will shadow team members, begin handling standard support requests independently, and establish yourself as a reliable technical resource for our Francophone customers.

By month 6 you'll be handling complex tickets across integrations, email infrastructure, and identity workflows independently. You'll understand how Hoxhunt fits into the broader security stack of our customers, start identifying patterns that inform product and process improvements, and become a trusted go-to expert for both our customers and internal teams.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You have:

 - Professional proficiency in French and English, with strong written and spoken communication skills and the ability to explain technical complexity clearly to a non-technical audience.

 - Prior experience in a technical support role, preferably with B2B SaaS products.

 - Strong troubleshooting instincts and a structured approach to diagnosing problems you haven't seen before.

 - Familiarity with email infrastructure, identity management, or enterprise IT environments — you don't need to be an expert in all of them, but you pick things up fast.

 - An appreciation for security and why it matters: you understand that the issues you resolve have a direct impact on how well organizations can protect themselves against real threats.

 - A genuine service mindset: you care about solving the customer's actual problem, not just closing the ticket.

 - Bonus points if you also have experience with cybersecurity products, IAM systems, cloud environments, or identity providers




WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH

You'll join a collaborative technical support team. The team

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.

Why this role matters

Most cybersecurity tools are built for machines. At Hoxhunt, we build for the humans using them.

Our incident response platform is experiencing rapid commercial growth and increased user adoption globally. The platform is a key security tool for our customers, used by Security Operations Center (SOC) teams daily in order to triage and automatically remediate malicious cyberattacks. When these workflows are intuitive, organizations are protected instantly and around the clock.

As a Senior Product Designer, your mission is to turn the complex, data-heavy reality of incident response into clarity and speed. You will own the user experience of our threat mitigation platform, leveraging cutting-edge AI tools to increase design throughput and ensuring security professionals have the fast, powerful tools they need to stop cybercrime.

What you’ll own and drive

 - Design and own the UI/UX of our incident response platform. You will create features that create an intuitive and clear experience for security operators helping them to their desired outcomes, bringing uncompromising attention to craft and highly detailed, pixel-level quality to the designs.

 - Improve and streamline complex, data-heavy user flows. You will review existing designs and turn legacy UI patterns into clear, reusable system components, balancing AI-assisted speed with exceptional polish and usability.

 - Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Developers, and a small, flat design team of 5 designers. You will move work forward with momentum - free of committee-based bottlenecks - ensuring your high-fidelity designs translate cleanly into production.

 - Validate and iterate concepts with cutting-edge AI tools for rapid prototyping. You will validate your design decisions with clear assumptions, collecting evidence and working with Product Analytics, customers and users rather than relying purely on intuition.

 - Contribute to our design system. You will help define core components, reduce design debt, and ensure closer code integration and consistency across the entire platform.

What success looks like

In your first three months, you will learn the core workflows of our incident response product, dive into our design system, and begin shipping UI improvements using rapid prototyping and AI tools.

By month six, you will be independently owning and streamlining complex, end-to-end user flows, making a high individual impact and visibly contributing to the overall design quality and consistency of the platform.

By month twelve, together with the team you have started to envision and work on the next product iteration of the incident response product, driving the UX principles and quality to boost adoption even further.

What makes you thrive here

You have:

 - Strong product design experience with a portfolio that showcases comprehensive design work - we want to see full flows, multiple screens, and actual pixel work.

 - Deep proficiency in Figma with a strong focus on complex SaaS product designs (mobile-only experience won't be sufficient for this role).

 - A proven ability to streamline complex user flows, designing for efficiency and intuitiveness in data-heavy environments.

 - A strong enthusiasm for AI tools in design - you are curious and experimental with them, eager to learn, and comfortable using tools for rapid prototyping.

 - A collaborative, team-player mindset. You are seeking high individual impact and ownership rather than hierarchical advancement.

 - A willingness to show us

source_jobs.title: Director of Customer Success
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.

About this Role
You are a highly operational customer success leader who thrives in a fast-paced scale-up environment. In this role, you will define the roadmap for our North American customer journey, ensuring that our clients successfully adopt, renew, and expand their use of our AI-native human risk management platform. You will not only manage and elevate a team of talented Customer Success Managers, but you will also roll up your sleeves to engage directly with our clients and stakeholders.



What You'll Do

 - Lead, mentor, and scale the Customer Success team in the US, fostering a culture of high performance, accountability, and continuous learning while delivering scalable growth across the customer base. Drive employee development through regular 1:1s, goal setting, coaching, performance reviews, and career growth conversations.

 - Own the regional gross and net revenue retention (GRR and NRR) targets, taking ultimate responsibility for customer health, renewals, and expansion opportunities.

 - Own and improve the regional customer journey for our US clients, ensuring seamless onboarding handoffs, rapid time-to-value, strong adoption, and long-term engagement.

 - Partner closely with our Sales, Product, and Marketing teams to align on go-to-market strategies and ensure customer feedback directly informs our product roadmap

 - Build upon and refine scalable playbooks, operational processes, and KPI frameworks that enable the Customer Success team to deliver exceptional experiences as our customer base grows.



What We're Looking For

 - Proven leadership experience of 3-4 years of managing and scaling Customer Success or Account Management teams within a B2B SaaS environment.

 - A strong track record of owning and exceeding gross and net revenue retention targets at a regional or organizational level.

 - Exceptional coaching and mentorship skills, with a passion for developing talent and building high-performing, collaborative teams.

 - Strategic operational mindset with the ability to build scalable processes, playbooks, and health-scoring frameworks from the ground up.

 - Excellent executive presence and communication skills, comfortable navigating complex enterprise accounts and building trust with senior leaders.

 - A highly autonomous, proactive approach to problem-solving, thriving in an ambiguous, fast-growth environment where you can take real ownership.

Bonus Points If You Also

 - Bring experience from the cybersecurity sector or a deep interest in human risk management.

 - Have successfully led teams through a significant scale-up phase or expansion into the North American market.
   
   

What you can expect from us:

 - Compensation: Annual base salary of $130,000 -140,000 depending on experience, plus a competitive commission model with an on-target earnings of $162,000 -$175,000

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, hybrid work setting. You are expected to collaborate from our Minneapolis office.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We bring an incredibly driven, high-impact energy to our work, but we remain deeply humble and team-focused. You will be surrounded by wildly talented, dedicated colleagues in an environment built on extreme kindness, support, and psychological safety.

 - Authentic trust & autonomy: We hire great people and trust them to do great work. You will find a culture free of micromanagement, giving you the autonomy to take real ownership, drive impact, and shape things early on.

 - A product you can be proud of: It is incredibly

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Analyst, Team Lead
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

In this role, you will shape how product analytics are done in a fast-growing, multi-product cybersecurity SaaS company with over 4 million end users. By acting as a player-coach—spending the vast majority of your time on hands-on individual contributor work while leading a team of three analysts—you will level up the team’s capabilities. Your work will transition our product analytics by bringing in experience and knowledge of SaaS product analytics that will directly impact product decisions and product strategy, and enable data-driven decision making across the organization.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN AND DRIVE

 - Lead the Product Analytics team, guiding members in SaaS product analytics methodologies while maintaining a 90% focus on hands-on IC work

 - Partner with Product Managers and product teams to conduct complex analyses that guide data-driven decision-making across all our products

 - Collaborate with the commercial organization to deliver high-impact insights for our largest enterprise customers

 - Improve and develop our core product analytics infrastructure, instrumentation, and team processes

 - Drive the team's transition from reactive request handling towards more proactive, outcome-oriented analytics


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your first 3 months you’ll get to know our products deeply, build strong collaborative relationships with Product Managers, and identify key improvement areas in our product analytics domain.

By month 6 you’ll successfully guide the team in a clear direction, level up our SaaS product analytics skills and capabilities and establish workflows that move the team from reactive mode of operation towards more proactive outcome-oriented projects.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You have:

 - At least 5 years of experience in the industry with a proven technical background in SaaS product analytics, ideally in companies with millions of end users or thousands of customer organizations

 - Deep, hands-on proficiency in SQL, Python programming, analytics engineering (such as dbt and data warehouses), and product analytics instrumentation

 - An outcome-oriented mindset where you are opinionated about metrics, know when not to do something, and focus heavily on decisions rather than just producing deliverables

 - Pragmatism, intellectual honesty under pressure, and a high comfort level with ambiguity and self-direction


BONUS POINTS IF YOU ALSO:

 - Bring direct experience working within a Product-Led Growth (PLG) business model

 - Have previously built instrumentation or an event taxonomy completely from scratch

 - Have a proven interest in and ability to act as a supportive team lead




WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

This role sits within our highly collaborative, fast-paced, three-person Product Analytics team which balances urgent customer needs with large-scale, proactive product analyses. We value pragmatism, intellectual honesty, and a genuine curiosity about how our users interact with our products. In this role, you’ll operate at the intersection of functions and teams, collaborating closely with Product Managers, development teams, data teams, and the commercial organization.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: We offer a market-competitive monthly salary based on your experience, skills, and impact, aligned with our internal leveling framework.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, but hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office at least 2-3 days per week.

 - High performance meets high huma

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, SMB, DACH
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.



WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

As a German-speaking Account Executive for the SMB market, you will play a crucial role in expanding Hoxhunt's footprint across the DACH region by focusing on organizations with up to 1000 employees. You will drive new business acquisition within this key segment, manage the full sales cycle, and contribute directly to our rapid growth in the region. This is an exciting opportunity to make a significant impact by bringing our award-winning solutions to a broad range of smaller and medium-sized businesses and helping them build a culture of cybersecurity.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN AND DRIVE

 - Drive Growth in the SMB Market: Be a key driver for Hoxhunt's growth across DACH region, specifically identifying and closing new business with organizations ranging up to 1000 employees.

 - Master the Full Sales Cycle with Inbound Support: Own and manage the entire sales process from prospecting and discovery (leveraging marketing inbounds and self-generated leads) to proposals, procurement, and closing with SMB clients in your segment.

 - Achieve Revenue Targets: Achieve and exceed your New Business ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) quota.

 - Become a Trusted Advisor: Build strong relationships and establish Hoxhunt’s credibility with key stakeholders, including decision-makers within your target accounts.

 - Collaborate for Success: Partner closely with our Marketing team to optimize inbound lead generation and execute a winning go-to-market strategy for the SMB market across DACH.

 - Represent Hoxhunt at industry events, trade shows, and customer meetings as needed across your region.

 - Contribute to building brand awareness and credibility within the SMB market globally.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In your first 3 months you'll: Craft a healthy, growing sales pipeline by leveraging marketing inbounds and self-generated opportunities, while building strong, trust-based relationships with key accounts in your segment.

 - By month 6 you'll: Secure significant new business ARR from SMB organizations (0-1000 employees) across the DACH region and contribute to Hoxhunt becoming a recognized key cybersecurity partner in the market.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You have:

 - 3+ years of experience as an Account Executive selling complex SaaS or software solutions, with a track record of exceeding targets.

 - Native or full professional proficiency in German, alongside fluency in English, preferably with professional experience selling to the DACH market.

 - Deep SMB Market Knowledge: Significant professional experience selling to Small and Medium-sized Businesses (0-1000 employees), with a strong understanding of their unique sales cycles and business needs.

 - Hunter Mentality & Drive: Goal-oriented, energetic, highly autonomous, and motivated by the challenge of acquiring new business within a competitive market.

 - Willingness to Travel: Based in Helsinki and eager to travel regularly across the DACH region for meetings, events, and networking as required.

 - Exceptional Organizational Skills: Capable of managing a high volume of meetings and open discussions while adapting strategies when faced with challenges.

Bonus points if you also:

 - Bring an existing network in the cybersecurity or IT sector within the SMB segment across the DACH region.




WHO YOU'LL WORK WITH

You will join a supportive, collaborative, and high-performing sales team focused on the EMEA region. Our Helsinki HQ will be your home base, with flexible opportunities for remote work once onboarded

As an Account Executive for France, you will be a vital force behind our continued expansion in this high-potential market. Joining our newly established French team, you will own the full sales cycle for strategic French enterprises, close significant new business ARR, and help establish Hoxhunt as the go-to solution for human risk management in cybersecurity. This is a high-impact opportunity to leverage our momentum, scale our presence in one of Europe's largest markets, and work alongside a team that is deeply passionate about our mission.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN AND DRIVE

• Drive Growth in France: Be a key driver for Hoxhunt's expansion, identifying and closing new business with strategic tier enterprises in the region.

• Hunter Mentality & Drive: Goal-oriented, energetic, and motivated by the challenge of scaling a market presence and winning new market share.

• Willingness to Travel: Based in Helsinki (we are open to supporting relocation within the EU for the right candidate) and eager to travel regularly to France for meetings, events, and networking.

• Nice-to-Have Edge: An existing network in the French cybersecurity or IT sector is a significant plus.




WHO YOU ARE

• a French speaker (full professional proficiency) who is also fluent in English.

• highly autonomous and self-motivated, you thrive in a scale-up environment where you take full ownership of your success while collaborating closely with your regional peers.

• ambitious and results-driven, with a passion for accelerating growth in a key market.

• skilled at establishing trust quickly and navigating complex enterprise procurement processes.

• equally comfortable with strategic deal-making and hands-on sales activities.

• resilient and resourceful, ready to adapt strategies when faced with challenges.

source_jobs.title: Channel Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We are looking for a Channel Account Manager to create strong, lasting channel relationships that amplify our reach, grow revenue, and ultimately help protect organizations and people from evolving cyber threats. This is more than a sales role. It is a chance to shape our U.S. channel strategy during a pivotal stage of growth.

As a Channel Account Manager, you will act as both a builder and a connector, cultivating strategic partnerships with resellers, enabling them with the tools to succeed, and ensuring our joint customers thrive. You will work closely with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to strengthen our go-to-market engine and position Hoxhunt as the partner of choice. You are a self-starter with a growth mindset, bringing a passion for solving real-world customer problems while expanding our regional footprint.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Develop and execute a regional channel strategy that supports revenue and growth targets.

 - Drive joint pipeline generation, partner enablement, and deal acceleration.

 - Build strong, lasting relationships with partner sales, technical, and executive teams.

 - Track partner performance, forecast pipeline, and report on key metrics.

 - Organize and manage partner events in your region.

 - Collaborate with direct sales on territory planning and joint opportunities.

 - Represent Hoxhunt at industry and partner events to expand regional presence and brand.

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In your first 30 days, quickly building trust and visibility across your territory. You’ll meet key AEs and SDRs, understand their partner relationships, and identify where to focus. You’ll begun mapping partner contacts and initiating outreach grounded in real opportunities and intent data. Early conversations are focused on learning, alignment, and uncovering quick wins.

 - By day 60, expanding engagement and creating early momentum with partners. You’re actively connecting with under-engaged but high-potential partners using tailored, value-driven outreach. You’re aligned with AEs and SDRs on target accounts and using that insight to drive meaningful partner conversations. Early joint planning sessions and initial deal activity are starting to take shape.

 - By day 90 and beyond, turning relationships into consistent pipeline. You’ve built strong connections with both active and underperforming partners and are engaging them through a mix of virtual and in-person touch points. Early conversations are progressing into deal registrations, and you’re refining your approach based on what’s working. You’re seen as a proactive, trusted partner internally and externally, contributing to sustained growth.

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of experience in channel sales or strategic alliances within SaaS, cybersecurity, or enterprise software.

 - Proven track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets in a partner-driven model.

 - Hands-on experience with partner lifecycle management, from recruitment to enablement.

 - Established partner network and knowledge of the regional channel landscape.

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills to influence stakeholders at all levels.

 


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience working in an early-stage, fast-growth SaaS company.

 - Cybersecurity experience.

 - Knowledge of Hoxhunt.

 


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer, SecOps
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

We're looking for a Security Engineer, SecOps to join Hoxhunt's Product Security team. Your core mission is to help mature our security monitoring into a stronger, automation-based security operations capability. We already have the foundations in place, and we want you to build on them with detection, observability and response delivered through tools and automation rather than a staffed SOC. You'll also coordinate vulnerability management across our engineering teams and write the production code and automation that makes security scale.



You'll also help keep our customer security answers accurate, working with the team on the security questionnaires and RFPs that unblock deals. This is a real and recurring part of the job, roughly 10–30% of your time depending on deal flow: reviewing and updating our existing security answers, and researching new ones where none exist yet, drawing on broad SOC 2, ISO and HIPAA knowledge, with the occasional customer call when a deal needs technical depth.



Beyond supporting deals, you'll also help turn customers' security and compliance requirements into product features and policy proposals, helping close the loop between what customers ask for and what we build. You won't start from scratch or do it alone: there's an existing library of security answers and compliance tooling (Vanta) behind you, and the team shares the load. The team's job is simple to state: address any security concern a product team or customer raises, and you'll be part of that.



This is an engineering role, not an analyst or shift-based one. You'll bring real, hands-on instinct for incident response and case management, and the job is to turn that instinct into systems: codifying it into automation, playbooks and tooling so detection and response scale without scaling headcount. Product Security is a small, high-leverage team: we work through discovery, planning and influence, and most implementation happens across the wider Technology organisation. You'll add the delivery muscle and the automation-first mindset to keep maturing our security roadmap (security observability, SIEM build-out, vulnerability management). We expect you to use modern AI tools throughout your work to expand and scale your reach.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN AND DRIVE

You’ll own security capabilities that help us detect, respond, and scale security through engineering.



Primary Responsibilities

 - Build and improve our security monitoring: own detections and alerting end-to-end (build, tune, maintain) and contribute to maturing our wider automation-based capability and SIEM build-out, with detection and response running through tools and code, not a manned SOC.

 - Build and operate our security observability: audit logging, security telemetry, and the dashboards/signals the rest of engineering relies on.

 - Coordinate vulnerability management end-to-end: triage, prioritise (CVSS plus exploitability and context), and drive remediation in partnership with the teams that own the code.

 - Strengthen our cloud security architecture: implement and help shape network segmentation and egress controls, IAM and least-privilege, secrets management and infrastructure-as-code on GCP, partnering with SRE/Platform.

 - Help connect customer requirements to what we build: turn security and compliance requirements from customers and frameworks into concrete feature and policy proposals, surfacing gaps to the team and roadmap.

 - Strengthen the secure development lifecycle: SAST, DAST, SCA, and s

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We are looking for a Sales Development Representative to join our team in the United States. This is a critical role in driving our international growth, helping us connect with new customers and expand our presence in a rapidly growing cybersecurity market.

As a Sales Development Representative at Hoxhunt, you’ll focus on generating new business opportunities through outbound prospecting and booking meetings for our Account Executives. You’ll work closely with both Sales and Marketing, building the foundation for pipeline growth while developing the skills needed to grow into a high-performing sales professional.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Generate pipeline through proactive outbound prospecting across phone, email, LinkedIn, and events.

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads, identifying strong opportunities for the sales team.

 - Book high-quality meetings for Account Executives to support revenue growth.

 - Partner closely with Marketing and Sales to align on messaging, campaigns, and target accounts.

 - Continuously refine your outreach approach based on feedback, data, and results.

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In your first 30 days, you'll learn Hoxhunt’s product, value proposition, and sales process while building strong prospecting habits.

 - By day 60, you'll start owning outbound activity, qualifying leads, and booking meetings with target accounts.

 - By day 90 and beyond, you'll consistently create pipeline by running effective outreach and partnering closely with Account Executives.

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Results-Oriented: You’re motivated by goals and have a track record of meeting or exceeding targets.

 - Curious Learner: You’re eager to grow your skills and develop a strong foundation in SaaS sales.

 - Proactive & Structured: You bring a focused, organized approach to your work and take initiative.

 - Strong Communicator: You’re articulate and confident across written and verbal channels.

 - Customer-Focused: You quickly understand business needs and connect them to value.

 - Relevant Experience (preferred): Exposure to cold calling, lead generation, or SaaS sales is a plus.

 


BONUS POINTS

 - Self-starter who takes ownership and follows through on commitments.

 - Ambitious and motivated to build a long-term career in sales or business development.

 - Resilient and adaptable, able to learn quickly and improve with feedback.

 - A team player who contributes to a positive, high-performing environment.

 


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

At Hoxhunt, we don’t just build software. We build behavior change on a global scale. With a world-class product and strong global growth, we help organizations create a culture of cybersecurity, one employee at a time.

You’ll join a collaborative team in our Minneapolis office, working closely with Account Executives, Marketing, and leadership. It’s a hands-on environment where you’ll gain visibility, build foundational sales skills, and make an impact early.

 
 


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: Annual base salary of $50k–$56k depending on experience, plus a competitive commission model with on-target earnings of $68k–$74k.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, hybrid setting. Most roles include regular in-office time, typically three days per week.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We’re a high-performing team with ambitious goals and a strong focus on impact. Just as important, we create an environment where people feel supported, respected, and empowered to succeed together.

 - Au

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive, APAC
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




YOUR MISSION

As an Enterprise Account Executive for the APAC region, your mission is to help large, complex organizations strengthen their human layer of security. Working with some of the region’s largest enterprises, you’ll partner with security leaders to solve one of today’s biggest cybersecurity challenges while helping grow Hoxhunt’s presence across APAC.




YOUR ROLE

We are looking for Enterprise Account Executive to join our sales team in Singapore. In this role, you will develop and execute territory strategies to drive revenue growth within Enterprise accounts, consistently meeting or exceeding quarterly sales targets.

You’ll proactively generate new business through outbound prospecting, navigate complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales cycles, and close high-value opportunities while representing Hoxhunt’s values. We will provide the tools, support, and frameworks to help you successfully grow our enterprise presence in the region. This role is ideal for someone who is highly autonomous, self-motivated, and thrives in a scale-up environment where they take full ownership of their success.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

WHY JOIN HOXHUNT?

 - Compensation: Competitive base salary depending on your experience, plus a competitive commission model.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, hybrid setting. You are expected to collaborate from our Singapore office.
