source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager, Marketing
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Hoxhunt is growing and we’re launching new products and shipping new capabilities at a rapid pace. The challenge isn’t building great products, it’s making sure the market understands them just as quickly.

We’re looking for a Product Marketing Manager to own how our products are brought to market and understood by buyers.

When we launch something new, you’ll ensure buyers understand it quickly across our website, launch communications and every major buyer touchpoint.

This role sits within the Marketing team and works closely with Product, Growth, Strategic Communications, Design, Sales and Customer Success. You’ll be the owner of Hoxhunt’s external product narrative - ensuring every new product, capability and solution is communicated clearly, consistently and at the pace we build.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN AND DRIVE

Product positioning & messaging

Turn technical product capabilities into clear customer value and business outcomes that buyers immediately understand.

Develop messaging frameworks that explain what each product does, who it’s for and why it’s different from competing solutions.

Partner closely with Product and Strategic Communications to ensure our positioning reflects customer needs, product innovation and market opportunities.

Product launches & go-to-market

Own the market-facing communication for every major product launch.

Work closely with Product teams to understand what’s changing and translate it into launch messaging and product pages.

Coordinate launch activities with Marketing, Product, Sales and Customer Success so every release reaches the market with clear, consistent and compelling messaging.

Product web experience

Own the market-facing messaging and narrative across Hoxhunt’s product, capability and solution pages.

Ensure our website reflects every product launch quickly and accurately, helping buyers understand what we’ve built and why it matters.

Continuously improve product pages through clearer messaging, stronger proof points and better buyer journeys.

Partner with Growth and Design to improve both conversion and discoverability across the product experience.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You have:

 - Experience in Product Marketing within B2B SaaS.

 - Exceptional writing and messaging skills with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts into compelling customer value.

 - Experience leading cross-functional product launches from planning through execution.

 - Strong experience developing product positioning, messaging and competitive differentiation.

 - Experience working closely with Product teams, designers and go-to-market functions.

 - Comfortable owning outcomes end-to-end instead of waiting for multiple approval rounds.

 - Bias toward shipping while maintaining high quality.

 - Curious, collaborative and able to move quickly in a fast-changing environment.

Bonus points if you also:

 - Experience in cybersecurity.

 - Experience working in a multi-product SaaS environment.

 - Experience developing competitive positioning and sales enablement.

 - Familiarity with how buyers increasingly research vendors through AI search, AI assistants and modern discovery platforms.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

Product innovation only creates growth when customers understand it.

Hoxhunt is expanding rapidly, launching new products and continuously evolving our platform. This role ensures our external product story keeps pace with that innovation, helping buyers clearly understand what we’ve built, why it matters

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Onboarding Manager
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

We are looking for a Customer Success Onboarding Manager for our Mid-Market whose mission is to ensure every new mid-market customer gets the right start—one that builds trust, removes friction, and sets them up for long-term success. You will play a pivotal role in guiding organizations through their initial Hoxhunt experience, turning complex technical implementations into seamless, value-driven onboarding journeys.

This role is designed for an experienced customer success or implementation professional who thrives in managing complex, high-stakes projects. You will own the full onboarding experience for our Mid-Market customers across the US, acting as their strategic guide and proactive point of contact from kick-off to go-live. You will navigate technical setups, align multiple stakeholders, and proactively address potential roadblocks to ensure projects stay on schedule. Beyond individual customer success, you will use your experience to refine our mid-market onboarding workflows, creating scalable processes that elevate our entire team.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the full onboarding experience for Mid-Market customers from kick-off to a successful go-live, ensuring a seamless and value-driven start.

 - Manage multiple complex onboarding projects simultaneously, setting clear goals, timelines, and expectations with key stakeholders.

 - Guide customers through technical setups, ensuring everything stays on track while proactively addressing potential roadblocks or technical challenges.

 - Serve as a reliable, strategic point of contact, spending significant time on video conducting high-value customer meetings.

 - Drive process improvements by refining onboarding workflows and documentation to help us scale and continuously elevate the customer experience.

 - Collaborate closely with Sales, Product, and broader Customer Success teams to ensure smooth handoffs and long-term customer success.
   
    


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years of experience in customer success, onboarding, technical implementation, or project management, ideally within B2B SaaS.

 - Proven experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder onboarding projects for mid-market or enterprise clients.

 - Strong technical aptitude, with the ability to confidently guide customers through technical setups and troubleshoot effectively.

 - Exceptional organizational and project coordination skills, with a disciplined approach to prioritizing tasks and meeting deadlines.

 - Excellent communication skills and a proactive, consultative mindset that builds immediate trust with customers.

 - A growth mindset, strong sense of ownership, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced scale-up environment.

 


BONUS POINTS

 - Hands-on experience with M365, G-Suite, SCIM, and SSO integrations.

 - Experience working within the cybersecurity industry.

 


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

At Hoxhunt, we don't just build software. We build behavior change on a global scale. With a world-class product and over 50% year-on-year growth, we're helping companies around the world create a culture of cybersecurity, one employee at a time.

You’ll join a high-performing team focused on growing our presence in North America. The Minneapolis office is a hub for collaboration, where people work closely together, share ideas openly, and support each other in getting results. It’s a hands-on environment where you’ll have visibility into the business and the opportunity to make an impact early.

 
 


WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Com

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (Italian-speaking)
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE

You already know what it’s like to work in sales development.

You’ve booked meetings.
You’ve handled rejection.
You’ve learned the basics of outbound.

What you’re looking for now is a role that helps you level up - in skill, confidence, and long-term career options.

At Hoxhunt, we’re growing rapidly in EMEA in a cybersecurity category that truly matters: human-centric security. We’re looking for an SDR who wants their next role to be more than just activity and targets - a role that builds strong foundations for future growth.




ABOUT HOXHUNT

Cybersecurity isn’t just about technology, it’s about human behavior.

Hoxhunt helps organizations turn employees into cyber defenders through behavior-driven training that actually changes how people act. Since 2016, we’ve grown into one of Europe’s fastest-growing SaaS scale-ups (growing over 50% year-on-year), trusted by over 4 million users worldwide.

Today, we’re a 270+ person global team across Finland, the US, and beyond, united by one purpose: empowering people to be the strongest link in cybersecurity.



Our culture is guided by three values we actually use in our day-to-day work:

 - Hungry for feedback

 - Courage to take initiative

 - We succeed together




THE ROLE

As a Sales Development Representative focusing on the Italian market, you’ll be the first point of contact for many of our future customers.

You’ll research accounts, engage CISOs and senior security leaders, and start high-quality conversations that turn into real sales opportunities. This role combines structured outbound work with hands-on exposure to how enterprise-level security conversations are built - from first touch to real, qualified pipeline.

You’ll be trained, supported, and challenged - and expected to grow.




WHAT THE TEAM SAYS:

"I always enjoy coming to the office, because my teammates are super supportive, funny, and everyone's genuinely invested in helping each other grow and succeed. With our team leads, it feels like they are truly on our side, always there to help us improve while showing us that our work is valued." - Aurelia, SDR, DACH

"The Hoxhunt SDR team is one of the most energetic and inspired teams you will likely come across. You are expected to perform, but supported in every way to succeed. Your colleagues are a tight-knit group that have each other's backs, with a "winning-together" atmosphere. If you're looking for an environment where your ambition can thrive, you should definitely apply!" - Gustav, Account Executive, former SDR, EMEA




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own outbound prospecting and pipeline creation for a defined set of Italian market accounts

 - Research target accounts and decision-makers using tools like ZoomInfo and LinkedIn Sales Navigator

 - Create personalized, relevant outreach across email, phone, LinkedIn, and events

 - Engage CISOs and security leaders in thoughtful, value-driven conversations

 - Collaborate closely with Account Executives to create qualified sales opportunities

 - Learn continuously through coaching, feedback, and experimentation


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 1–2 years of experience as an SDR or BDR, preferably in SaaS

 - Native-level Italian and fluent English

 - Experience with cold calling and multi-channel outbound

 - A coachable, resilient, and ambitious mindset

 - Curiosity about cybersecurity and complex B2B sales

 - Familiarity with the Italian market is a strong plus

Nice to have: experience with tools like ZoomInfo, Sales Navigator, or similar.




YOUR GROWTH A

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, New product, Front-end
source_jobs.description:
OUR MISSION AND WHY IT MATTERS

We are on a mission to make humans the strongest security layer.

Human risk remains one of the biggest vulnerabilities and traditional awareness training is not enough. We take a different approach by combining AI-driven personalization, real threat detection, and behavioral science to actively protect people and organizations.

We don't just simulate risks. We build the tools that detect and stop them.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

In this role you're building Hoxhunt's new product, helping organizations to see, understand, and act on human risks.

Your mission is to make those insights clear and actionable. You’ll focus on admin and the end-user experience, adding new features and improving the parts that matter most - data visualizations, user flows, and the automation builder.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN AND DRIVE

Together with the team, own and ship the UX end-to-end - from customer problem to solution ideas, architecture and implementation.

Lead frontend architecture of our new product and evolve the design system. Implement API/schema changes when it unblocks the work. Tech stack: TypeScript/Node.js, AlloyDB, GraphQL, React.

Decide what to build by understanding the user problem and the business impact. Evaluate tradeoffs and use real data to iterate.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In the first 90 days, you've shipped meaningful features end-to-end and you're influencing product and technical decisions.

By month six, you own the frontend architecture and the product UX. Your judgment, alongside customer feedback, shapes the roadmap.




WHAT MAKES YOU THRIVE HERE

You’ve led the frontend development across multiple projects, probably with 5+ years of experience, but we care more about what you've built than the years.

Having expertise in React and TypeScript, you’re strong on frontend architecture, accessibility, and performance. You produce world-class UX with our designer.

Genuinely care about the product and its users. Under ambiguity, you’ll collaborate with the whole team to figure out the right things rather than wait for a spec.

Experience with AI coding tools, or you're interested in building the setup that makes them effective for you.

Bonus points if you also have:

 - Figma fluency; design system / component library experience.

 - Complex data UX, data viz, or motion/micro-interactions.

 - Comfortable going full-stack when needed (GraphQL, backend integration)




WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

You will be joining a small, mission-driven, and highly autonomous product team: six engineers, one designer, one PM.

We own our new product end-to-end. We want ship fast in small increments: something a customer can use now, then iterate.




WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US

 - Compensation: The monthly salary is between €6,000-7,500/month depending on your on experience. You may notice varying salary ranges for roles with similar titles across Hoxhunt; this is because each range is grounded in our role leveling and reflects the seniority, scope, and impact expectations required for that specific role and team, and we operate with a low hierarchy.

 - Working ways: We work in a flexible, but hybrid work setting. You are expected to visit the Helsinki office weekly.

 - High performance meets high humanity: We bring an incredibly driven, high-impact energy to our work, but we leave our egos at the door. You will be surrounded by wildly talented, dedicated colleagues in an environment built on extreme kindness, support, and psychological safety.

 - Authentic trust & autonomy: We hire great people and trust them to do great work. You will find a culture free of micromanagement, giving you the autonomy to take real ownership, drive impact, and shape things early on.

 - A product you can be proud of: It is incredibly rare in cybersecurity to build a product that end-users genuinely love. You will join a fast-paced, technically sophisticated team making a real, measurable impact against cybercrime, backe

notes: High-growth cybersecurity SaaS company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
